seg = current_frame.segmentation

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 20:
        boundary = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
        cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows[{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols[{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], children={n['children']}")

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nLeft c-frame area:")
for i in range(32, 37):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][8:22]}")

print("\nGap area:")
for i in range(28, 35):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][26:42]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so after the second RIGHT move:
1. The left c-frame is now complete again (16 pixels, cols 11-15)
2. The white square is back inside the c-frame (cols 12-14)
3. The left agent moved from cols 15-17 to cols 18-20 (its original starting position!)
4. The left agent has W center
5. The right agent is still at cols 30-32 with c center
So the pattern seems to be:
- First RIGHT: kicks the left agent out of the c-frame, breaks the c-frame wall
- Second RIGHT: restores the c-frame and moves the left agent back to its original position
Wait, but the left agent was at cols 15-17 after the first RIGHT, and now it's at cols 18-20. So it moved RIGHT by 3 cells. And the c-frame was restored.
Hmm, so maybe pressing RIGHT when the active agent can't move (blocked by purple bar) causes the OTHER agent to move right? Let me test this theory.
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The right agent is blocked by the purple bar when trying to go RIGHT. When I press RIGHT:
1. First time: left agent gets kicked out of c-frame (moves from 12-14 to 15-17), c-frame wall breaks
2. Second time: left agent moves from 15-17 to 18-20, c-frame restores
So the left agent is moving RIGHT each time I press RIGHT (when the right agent is active and blocked). The left agent started at 18-20, went into the c-frame at 12-14, then got pushed to 15-17, then to 18-20.
Wait, but 18-20 is the left agent's original starting position! So after two RIGHT presses, the left agent returned to its starting position and the c-frame was restored.
This is getting complex. Let me think about what the goal might be. Maybe:
1. The goal is to move the left agent into the left c-frame AND the right agent into the right c-frame
2. The right agent can't physically reach the right c-frame
3. But when the right agent is active and presses RIGHT (blocked), it moves the left agent
4. Maybe I need to use this mechanic to position the left agent correctly
Or maybe the goal is completely different. Let me reconsider.
Actually, I wonder if the goal is to have BOTH agents inside their respective c-frames simultaneously. The left agent is currently at 18-20 (outside the c-frame), and the right agent is at 30-32 (can't reach right c-frame).
Let me try a different approach. What if I:
1. Click the left agent to activate it
2. Move it into the left c-frame
3. Click the right agent to activate it
4. Move it RIGHT repeatedly to push the left agent out, then... hmm
Actually, I think I need to understand the mechanic better. Let me try pressing RIGHT one more time to see the pattern.
